unmatched_source_examples: Registration Citizens can register their complaints with police and then based on the evidence, facts and following investigation, police shall take the complaint forward.
The solution should provide detailed context-sensitive help material for all the possible actions and scenarios on all user interfaces in the application.
The solution should send alerts (e.g., email, SMS) to the user if the user chooses to whenever any action has been taken on the alert.
The solution should enable the user to track the submitted defect or enhancement request.
The solution should enable the help-desk user to view the reports on the submitted defects or enhancement requests category-wise, status-wise, and age- wise.

The System must keep an unalterable audit trail capable of automatically capturing and storing information about: All the actions (create/read/update/delete) that are taken upon the critical entities (case, suspect, property,...) in the system The user initiating and or carrying out the action;
it may be subject to re-department and copying to removable media if required, so long as its contents remain unchanged.
Once the audit trail functionality has been activated, the System must track events without manual intervention, and store in the audit trail information about them.
The System must maintain the audit trail for as long as required, which will be at least for the life of the case to which it refers.
The System must at a minimum be able to provide reports for actions on cases organised: By case;
The System should be able to provide reports for actions on cases organised by workstation and (where technically appropriate) by network address.
The System must allow the user to limit access to cases to specified users or user groups.
The System must allow only admin-users to set up user profiles and allocate users to groups.
The System should allow a user to stipulate which other users or groups can access cases.

If a user requests access to, or searches for, a case which he does not have the right to access, the System must provide one of the following responses (selectable at configuration time): display title and metadata;
unmatched_generated_examples: Users can place orders with minimal steps, adding items to their cart, proceeding to checkout, and completing payment without too many interactions.
Users can check their order status by logging in or entering an order number, then viewing the current status page which displays all relevant information like delivery date and tracking details.
Users should not have to re-enter the same information every time they place an order; data from previous interactions can be pre-filled.
When a customer logs in and starts a new order, the system should recognize them and auto-populate fields like shipping address and payment method where applicable.
The system should allow users to view and confirm or change any saved payment details securely.
The system should handle scenarios where users might place multiple orders simultaneously, allowing them to add several items to their cart and checkout all at once.
The system should manage a list of items across different orders, enabling users to easily switch between them without losing any items.
The system should provide clear instructions on how to correct input errors.
Text resizing should make it easier on the eyes and improve readability, especially in long articles or detailed instructions like those found in the Help Module.
Alternative text-only pages should be provided to allow users to skim through long text pages without getting distracted by hyperlinks.
The system should clearly indicate which step a user is on and how many steps remain when filling out a form for creating an account.
Screen readers should be able to navigate through the application smoothly without any issues.
The system should minimize the number of steps needed to reach any piece of content while respecting different mental models and tasks.
The system should automatically validate user-defined default values against specific International Standards.
User-defined default values must comply with legal requirements for evidence collection and investigation.

match_examples.source_requirement: If a user performs a quick or advanced search, the System must never include in the search result list any record which the user does not have the right to access.
match_examples.generated_requirement: When a user performs a quick or advanced search, the system must not include any records in the result list that they do not have access to.

match_examples.source_requirement: Unplanned downtime for the System must not exceed <xx hours/minutes> per <rolling three month period>.
match_examples.generated_requirement: Unplanned downtime for the system must not exceed twice in any rolling three-month period.

match_examples.source_requirement: The System must be able to export audit trails for specified cases (without affecting the audit trail stored by the System).
match_examples.generated_requirement: Audit trails must be exported for specific cases without affecting the stored data, allowing later review.

match_examples.source_requirement: The planned downtime for the System must not exceed <xx> hours per <rolling three month period>.
match_examples.generated_requirement: Planned downtime for the system should not exceed 8 hours in any rolling three-month period.

match_examples.source_requirement: Allowing users to see and change profiles: If user-specified profiles are used, users should be able to see, modify and delete that profile on demand.
match_examples.generated_requirement: Users should have the ability to see, modify, and delete their own profiles as needed.

match_examples.source_requirement: Linking back to the home page or landmark pages: Each page should contain a link leading to the home page of the application or to a landmark page that is easy to recognize for the user.
match_examples.generated_requirement: Each page should have a link back to the home or a recognizable landmark page.

match_examples.source_requirement: The System must allow changes to security attributes for groups or users (such as access rights, security level, privileges, password allocation and management) to be made only by super-user.
match_examples.generated_requirement: Super-users should be able to change security attributes like access rights, security levels, privileges, and password management.

match_examples.source_requirement: The System must be able to retrieve and display within 5-8 seconds the case which has been accessed within the previous 2 months, regardless of storage capacity or number of cases in the system.
match_examples.generated_requirement: The system should retrieve and display cases accessed within the last two months within 5-8 seconds.

match_examples.source_requirement: Placing title information consistently: Page titles should be placed in a consistent location on the different pages.
match_examples.generated_requirement: Page titles should be consistently placed in a prominent location to help users understand where they are and what information is available.

match_examples.source_requirement: The System must be able to retrieve and display within 20 seconds the case which has not been accessed within the previous 2 months, regardless of storage capacity or number of cases in the system.
match_examples.generated_requirement: For older cases not accessed in the past two months, retrieval and display should happen within 20 seconds.

match_examples.source_requirement: Placing navigation components consistently: Navigation components should be placed consistently on the pages or in the framesets in the pages of the application.
match_examples.generated_requirement: Navigation components like menus and links should be consistently placed across the application.

match_examples.source_requirement: Minimizing navigation effort: The number of navigation steps needed to reach a certain piece of content should be minimized as long as different mental models, navigation strategies and tasks of the user are taken into account.
match_examples.generated_requirement: Navigation should be streamlined to minimize the number of steps needed to reach any piece of content while respecting different mental models and tasks.

match_examples.source_requirement: Text quality: The quality of textual content with respect to spelling and grammar should be sufficient so as not to impede readability.
match_examples.generated_requirement: The system should ensure top-notch text quality with impeccable spelling and grammar to improve readability.

match_examples.source_requirement: If a splash screen is used, a navigation option to skip it should be offered.
match_examples.generated_requirement: Splash screens should be bypassable using a skip link to facilitate navigation for users with visual impairments.

match_examples.source_requirement: Providing text equivalents for non-text media objects: All non-text media objects, such as graphical images or video, should be provided with alternative equivalent textual descriptions and/or with equivalent text-based functionality.
match_examples.generated_requirement: All non-text media objects should be appropriately described with text equivalents.
